channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Senate confirms Trump nominee for chairman of Joint Chiefs of Staff in overnight vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan “Razin” Caine to become the next chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff on Friday, filling the position almost two months after President Donald Trump fired his predecessor. Trump nominated Caine to become the top U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6, including family of Spanish tourists
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A US Navy captain ordered a military funeral for a kamikaze pilot during WWII. Here's why
item.description: PEARL HARBOR, Hawaii (AP) — A Japanese pilot slammed his Zero fighter plane into the USS Missouri and ignited a fireball on April 11, 1945, during the Battle of Okinawa. The suicide attack instantly killed the pilot, but none of the battleship's crew members were badly hurt.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In fight over insurance, neighbors crowdsource LA fire contamination data
item.description: All sense of survivors’ guilt was fleeting for those residents whose homes remained standing after wildfires ripped through the Los Angeles area three months ago.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER and SALLY HO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pro-Palestinian demonstrators who occupied Stanford building are charged with felonies
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — A dozen pro-Palestinian demonstrators who were arrested at Stanford University last year after they occupied and allegedly caused hundreds of thousands of dollars in damage to a campus building are now facing charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No, Casey Anthony did not open a Branson daycare
item.description: Google Trends showed a variety of topics being searched in Missouri on Thursday morning---sports, weather, the lottery, and one specific question: Did Casey Anthony open a daycare in Branson, Missouri?
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized state party in swing state is leaving
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized the party in the presidential swing state announced Thursday he will be leaving his position in June after a six-year run that included a string of victories and “agonizingly close” losses.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Grand jury indicts man for threats against Augusta National employee
item.description: A Georgia grand jury has returned an indictment against a man for his role in making threats against an employee of the Augusta National Golf Club.
item.dc:creator: D.V. Wise, Fox 5

item.title: GiveSendGo defends fundraiser for track meet stabbing suspect
item.description: Karmelo Anthony, 17, was arrested in the fatal stabbing of another Texas high school student, Austin Metcalf.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US stocks dive as euphoria on Wall Street reverts to fear about US-China trade war
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. stocks dove Thursday and surrendered a chunk of their historic gains from the day before as President Donald Trump’s trade war continues to threaten the economy.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge will halt Trump administration from ending humanitarian parole for people from four countries
item.description: A federal judge says she will halt the Trump administration from ending a program that allowed Cubans, Haitians, Nicaraguans and Venezuelans to temporarily live in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meet the Gulf shrimpers rooting on Trump's tariffs in a Texas fishing town
item.description: While American consumers and markets wonder and worry about President Donald Trump’s on-again, off-again tariffs, Gulf coast shrimpers are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: Lekan Oyekanmi And Jim Vertuno, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man arrested for 'economic interruption' after dump truck crash causes Draper power outage
item.description: Police arrested a man for allegedly knocking down power poles while driving a dump truck with the lift gate up on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Mourners pay tribute to merengue icon Rubby Pérez, who died in the Dominican roof collapse
item.description: Fans and countrymen on Thursday shared their remembrances of Dominican Republic music icon Rubby Pérez, who was among the scores of people killed this week after the roof at the Jet Set club in Santo Domingo collapsed. He was 69.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Helicopter crashes in New York City's Hudson River; 6 dead
item.description: A Bell 206 helicopter belonging to a tour company crashed in the water near Jersey City and Hoboken around 3:15 p.m., according to NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, Fox 5

item.title: House Democrats warn Musk must leave government role by May 30
item.description: “In his short time in government, Elon Musk has done enormous harm to working Americans,” House Democrats said.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: The order applies to all regulations issued under laws governing things like energy appliance standards, mining and offshore drilling — as well as regulations issued under the Endangered Species Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens to put higher tariff rate back if countries don’t strike deals
item.description: President Trump on Thursday threatened to reverse course on a 90-day pause on country-specific tariffs if countries don’t strike a deal with his administration before then.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: St. George parents arrested after toddler found alone outside
item.description: Two parents in St. George were taken into custody on Wednesday after their 2-year-old son was found outside of the home unattended.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Egg prices jump to record high despite Trump's predictions and bird flu slowing
item.description: The increase reported Thursday in the Consumer Price Index means consumers and businesses that rely on eggs should not anticipate immediate relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: This federal rule helped clear air over America's most beloved parks. Trump's EPA wants to kill it
item.description: During a hike in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park in 1995, Don Barger climbed Chilhowee Mountain hoping to gaze across the valley below. All he saw was a wall of gray haze. Today, he said, he can see some 50 miles (80 kilometers) across that same valley to the Cumberland Mountains.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ballerina Ksenia Karelina freed from Russian custody in prisoner exchange
item.description: Ksenia Karelina, a U.S.-Russian dual national who was detained last year on treason charges, was released from Russia in the latest prisoner exchange, according to the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge to order Dartmouth student's visa be reinstated
item.description: A federal judge is set to order the visa reinstatement of a Chinese doctoral student at Dartmouth College after it was inexplicably revoked by Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas lottery player claims he was cheated out of $95 million jackpot win
item.description: A Texas lottery player filed a lawsuit in Travis County on Tuesday alleging his May 2023 Lotto Texas jackpot prize was severely diminished by $95 million because a group of lottery retailers and a London-based sports gambling company conspired to rig a lottery drawing less than a month before his wi
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, Fox 5

item.title: Exclusive: Pence agrees Trump’s tariff policy a potential midterm liability for GOP
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the Trump administration’s aggressive use of tariffs poses a potential risk for Republicans in the midterm elections, even as he expressed relief about a walk-back of some of the most severe tariffs on allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: VP of Bikers Against Child Abuse chapter arrested for sex crimes against children
item.description: He was charged with promoting a sexual performance by a child and possessing a sexual performance by a child.
item.dc:creator: Kayleigh Hunter-Gasperini, NewsNation

item.title: 1,700 pounds of butter recalled over bacteria contamination
item.description: Nearly a ton of salted butter is being recalled because, according to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, the product is "contaminated with elevated levels of coliform," a bacteria found in animals' digestive tracts and waste.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sam's Club plans to open 15 new locations a year, CEO says
item.description: Sam's Club CEO Chris Nicholas says the company plans to open 15 new locations a year "for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp
item.description: The increase for a “forever” stamp, postcards, metered letters and international mail would take effect this summer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Vice president of Bikers Against Child Abuse chapter arrested for sex crimes against children
item.description: New York State Police said the vice president of Buffalo's Bikers Against Child Abuse chapter has been arrested for sex crimes against children.
item.dc:creator: Kayleigh Hunter-Gasperini, WFLA, NBC

item.title: She survived being shot in the head as teen. Now she's sharing her final message
item.description: Deserae Turner — who survived being shot in the head in 2017 and has undergone several medical procedures since — is now in hospice after recent health complications.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KRON4

item.title: Dramatic video captures firefighter being pulled from rubble after home explosion
item.description: Dramatic body camera footage captured the moments an Illinois home exploded, trapping a firefighter underneath the rubble.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Didn't get a Trader Joe's mini pastel tote? You can buy one for over $1,000 on eBay
item.description: And as they did last year, customers were quick to snag the bags. If you weren't among them, you're in luck — but only if you're willing to spend a pretty penny.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KRON4

item.title: LeBron James becomes first pro male athlete to have likeness depicted in Ken doll
item.description: LeBron James has become the first professional male athlete to have his likeness depicted in a Ken doll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The world's most famous YouTuber slams Trump's tariff war
item.description: Entrepreneur and YouTube star MrBeast slammed President Trump’s “brutal” tariffs in a post on the social platform X on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: Huntsman Cancer Institute breaks ground on new $400M Utah County cancer center
item.description: The Huntsman Cancer Institute broke ground on its new location in Utah County. Here's the project details and timeline.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4
